text:
Assistant: **What the amendment is:** ADR-0075 is the decision that lets NMP use Trellis internally ("private reconciliation substrate") — and its Boundaries section flatly forbids Trellis types in *any* app/native/web-facing API, enforced by doctrine-lint and the leak ratchet. That rule has no carve-out, so `nmp-devtools` — which by definition exposes Trellis trace/audit receipts to tooling — would be a doctrine violation the day it's written. The amendment (#2809) adds a second category to the ADR: a **diagnostic surface** — dev-build-only, never linked by app code, compiled out of release artifacts — where Trellis vocabulary may legally appear. Without it, every phase of the X-Ray epic is fighting the linter; with it, the lint gate knows exactly what's exempt.

**Yes, I can do it** — the writing is mechanical once one genuine architecture question is settled, which the Fable review flagged as a back-edge: the exemption wording depends on *how receipts reach Chirp*. That's your call:

Assistant: **No call needed anymore — the amendment already landed while we were talking.**

**What it is:** ADR-0075 is the ruling that lets NMP use Trellis as its private reconciliation substrate, with a hard boundary: no Trellis vocabulary in any app/native/web-facing surface, enforced by doctrine-lint. The amendment (#2809) adds a second surface category — a **diagnostic surface**: a dev-build-only `nmp-devtools` crate that *may* see Trellis trace/audit data, is never linked by app code, and is compiled out of release builds. Without it, the entire X-Ray epic would be a standing lint violation; with it, the exemption is explicit and machine-checkable. [...] - **PR #2859** — the ADR amendment itself, closing #2809, with the lint classifier scoped to `crates/nmp-devtools/src` and a regression test. **Phase A: done.** [...] **5. [Should-fix — Phase A] "Compiled out of release builds" needs a mechanical definition and CI enforcement.** `cfg(debug_assertions)` is the wrong tool (release-with-debug builds exist; per-platform build configs differ). Suggested wording: nmp-devtools is a separate crate behind an off-by-default cargo feature; CI asserts each platform's release artifact dependency graph (`cargo tree`) and/or symbol table contains no nmp-devtools; the ratchet rule is dependency-graph-based — an allowlist file of crates permitted to depend on `trellis-*` (existing private adapters + nmp-devtools), where adding a line is the ratchet violation. [...] **4. [Blocker — Phase A/D sequencing] The lint exemption can't be finalized before the Phase D transport is decided.** Phase D leaves "declared projection **or** devtools channel" open. If receipts ride the normal FlatBuffer projection pipeline, the receipt schema becomes part of the (dev-build) app surface and the Phase A lint/ratchet wording must account for that; if it's a side channel, the exemption is purely dependency-graph-shaped. Suggested change: move the transport decision into Phase A / #2809 as a decision-gate item. [...] **Other findings that changed the epic:** capsules need a **symbolication manifest** (traces are keyed by opaque NodeIds — unreadable to anyone who didn't see the session without an embedded label registry); Trellis trace versioning is exact-match-only, so the capsule needs its own **envelope version + compat policy** or every capsule attached to an old issue dies on the first format bump; the Phase D **transport decision moved into Phase A** because the lint-exemption wording depends on it; free-form reason strings became **structured reason codes** (type lints can't catch a Debug-formatted `ResourcePlan` leaking into a string); the receipt shape gained the fields its own examples required (owner-count *transition*, outcome status, parent scope, timestamps); my "agent answers why-is-my-feed-empty" exit criterion was rightly called untestable and is now a **seeded failure-scenario corpus** against a local relay fixture — deliberately including the "subscription fine, zero events came back" case, which receipts alone can't answer; and the **headless NMP host runner** is now itemized as its own (probably largest) Phase C work item. [...] 2. **X-Ray (the critical-path decision gate):** *"Work Phase A of NMP epic #2858: draft the ADR-0075 amendment for #2809 — define the diagnostic surface, decide the Phase D transport (dev-only declared projection vs side devtools channel) and record it in the ADR, and implement the doctrine-lint allowlist ratchet (crates permitted to depend on `trellis-*`) plus the string-leak snapshot test."* Everything else in that epic queues behind this ADR.
